channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In the Ebola epicenter, a gold-mining town reacts with fear, disbelief and grit
item.description: NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.
item.content:encoded:
NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.

(Image credit: Arsène Mpiana for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone

item.title: Why are crisis pregnancy centers saying they can 'rule out' ectopic pregnancy?
item.description: Under President Trump, more federal attention and support has gone towards anti-abortion Christian centers. A watchdog group says many of them mislead patients with promises to "rule out" ectopic pregnancies.
item.content:encoded:
Under President Trump, more federal attention and support has gone towards anti-abortion Christian centers. A watchdog group says many of them mislead patients with promises to "rule out" ectopic pregnancies.

(Image credit: Taylor Glascock for The Washington Post)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: How to protect yourself from ticks
item.description: It's tick season, but how do you avoid the little arachnids? NPR asks two experts in tick bites and behavior.
item.content:encoded: It's tick season, but how do you avoid the little arachnids? NPR asks two experts in tick bites and behavior.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Red Cross volunteers work to combat heat-related deaths at Phoenix mobile homes
item.description: In Phoenix, Ariz., a disproportionate number of indoor heat-related deaths happen in mobile homes. Red Cross volunteers are working to change that.
item.content:encoded: In Phoenix, Ariz., a disproportionate number of indoor heat-related deaths happen in mobile homes. Red Cross volunteers are working to change that.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Davis-Young

item.title: Trouble getting weight loss drugs covered by insurance? Here's what to know
item.description: If your doctor prescribes a GLP-1 medication for weight loss but your insurance won't cover it, you have options.
item.content:encoded:
If your doctor prescribes a GLP-1 medication for weight loss but your insurance won't cover it, you have options.

(Image credit: Oona Zenda)
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin

item.title: A look at the science that's been lost on long COVID
item.description: Since President Trump took office, hundreds of experts advising on science and research issues have been dismissed. One committee was on long COVID, an issue that doctors are desperate for help with.
item.content:encoded: Since President Trump took office, hundreds of experts advising on science and research issues have been dismissed. One committee was on long COVID, an issue that doctors are desperate for help with.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: 8 things to know about the gut microbiome and keeping yours healthy
item.description: Wellness influencers often talk about fixing a broken gut microbiome. And marketers sell tests and supplements to fix your gut health. Here's what what the evidence really shows about gut health.
item.content:encoded: Wellness influencers often talk about fixing a broken gut microbiome. And marketers sell tests and supplements to fix your gut health. Here's what what the evidence really shows about gut health.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Despite state bans, abortions have almost doubled. The reason? Pills via telehealth
item.description: States that have banned abortion are suing to stop mailing of abortion pills over state lines. But the telehealth providers say no matter the outcome, they can adapt, and so will their patients.
item.content:encoded:
States that have banned abortion are suing to stop mailing of abortion pills over state lines. But the telehealth providers say no matter the outcome, they can adapt, and so will their patients.

(Image credit: Charles Krupa)
item.dc:creator: Kate Wells

item.title: Despite state bans, abortions have almost doubled in the U.S.
item.description: Four years ago, the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade. Yet there are nearly twice as many abortions in the U.S. as before. That's because abortion pills are now being prescribed online and mailed.
item.content:encoded: Four years ago, the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade. Yet there are nearly twice as many abortions in the U.S. as before. That's because abortion pills are now being prescribed online and mailed.
item.dc:creator: Kate Wells

item.title: A blood test that screens for multiple types of cancer? It may be available soon
item.description: The FDA could approve the first blood test that can detect multiple cancers, around the end of the year, setting the stage for it to become widely available.
item.content:encoded:
The FDA could approve the first blood test that can detect multiple cancers, around the end of the year, setting the stage for it to become widely available.

(Image credit: Anand Purohit/Moment RF)
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi
